content:
When I was younger, I genuinely believed I could build enough wealth by the time I turned 30, retire early, and spend the rest of my life doing whatever I wanted.
The internet makes that dream feel realistic.
Every day you see someone claiming, “If I knew what I know today five years ago, I’d already be a millionaire.” Or someone saying they retired at 32 after building a startup or investing in crypto.
It creates the impression that working until your 60s is optional.
But after sitting down and actually doing the math, I realized how far that is from reality.
The biggest mistake I made was assuming the future would look like today.
Think back to 2020.
Almost nobody predicted how quickly AI would go Stonks 📈📈. Software engineers were among the most sought-after professionals. Companies were hiring aggressively and demand seemed endless.
AI now writes code, generates documentation, creates designs, and automates work that used to take teams of people.
Any financial plan that assumes your income will keep growing forever, your industry will stay the…
